“My biggest loss ever was actually on Ethereum chain, I lost $35,000 on one token on Ethereum. "

"I have taken big loss on Sol recently. That was like 6.6 Solana which is big for my wallet. That was a big loss. And I think he changed his profile picture or something and like instantly rugged. 

"Elon coins are ruthless. Now I'm avoiding completely the Elon coins.”
 
“So I learned that lesson and I just cut the loss and didn't play another Elon profile picture token. But it still happens. I still take pretty bad losses."

"Recently I had the big loss on a $200-300K market cap shitcoin. My dumbest idea. I was like oh I'm going to sleep on it and it's going to stay overnight. This was three weeks ago and that was the last time I did overnights. I said okay now my rule is I'm never doing overnight again because the coin dumped 80%. So it was a 10 Sol position and I lost 8 Sol. It was almost one week two weeks of profits gone in one position which is always so hard to take.”

🌟 Highlighted Notable Solana Projects in the Past Week 

🔸@UmbraPrivacy is bringing private transfers and swaps to Solana. Its mainnet push adds encrypted, unlinkable transactions for users who want onchain privacy.
🔸@PlayBabylon is now open to everyone on Solana. Humans and AI agents can compete in a multiplayer prediction game with agent identity and reputation on-chain.
🔸@BagsApp is pushing its Solana social-fi app forward. The project is leaning into a community-driven experience with a possible $BAGS airdrop narrative.
🔸@SquadsLabscontinues to build core multisig infrastructure on Solana. It supports institutional-grade asset management for teams and treasuries.
🔸@orca_so is expanding beyond swaps with Wavebreak on Solana. The human-first launchpad adds stronger anti-bot protection for new launches.
🔸@BedrockFndn’s BR token is now live on Solana. Users can bridge from BSC via InterportFi, secured by Chainlink CCIP.
🔸@hinkal_protocol is developing private payments for Solana. The protocol is moving toward confidential settlement without changing the user’s existing wallet flow.
🔸@RaiseApp and Solana Payments are making USDC payments smoother. Users can pay in stablecoins while apps handle the gas experience behind the scenes.
🔸@EitherwayAI turns ideas into full-stack Solana apps with one prompt. Generate the stack, deploy mainnet-ready code, and ship monetizable dApps instantly.
🔸@playsolana is expanding its gaming ecosystem with Playverse on PSG1. Users can discover and play Solana-native games directly on the handheld device.

The rise and fall of Tensor

2022:
> two ex-quant traders with no funding
> win two Solana hackathons back to back
> bootstrap with $60k prize money
> launch during the FTX collapse
> zero users, zero revenue, zero social presence

2023:
> raise $3M
> quietly become #1 NFT marketplace on Solana
> beat Magic Eden in volume
> launch Tensorians NFT at 1.69 SOL

late 2023:
> Solana season begins
> pioneering cNFTs
> Tensorians hit 85 SOL (50x from mint)
> capturing 85% of all Solana NFT volume
> the "Blur of Solana" narrative is real

2024:
> TNSR token launches over $1B FDV
> everyone celebrates
> volumes go down
> farmers leave Tensor for Magic Eden

2025:
> NFT market dies
> daily volume down 80% from peak
> $20k daily fees
> team builds a new platform (Vector)
> pivot more and more to memecoins
> Vector adopts $TNSR (new hope)

2025 (Coinbase acquisition):
> Coinbase acquired Vector just to shut it down
> Coinbase takes over core team
> Tensor and $TNSR token not part of the deal
> $TNSR pumped 8-11x before announcement
> founder allocations were relocked for additional 3 years

2026:
> Tensor foundation leads marketplace now
> last tweet 4 months ago
> revenue down to $250/day
> future unclear...

reason: DeFi Yield / LP cluster linked by [pool, yield, solana, token, volume]

sampleTweets.text:
80085-sol, three times. $35, $42, $149. same token, same strat, just kept farming it.

narrative was great last night and the token survived, so i kept going. volume was sitting at 200k-350k per 5 minutes, TVL was low, pool was on 5% base fee. that combination is exactly what you want for spot tight. 

pool link in the comments if you want to farm this too.

the strat is spot -10%, single side sol. the exit rule is strict: when price touches about half of my position and starts going out of range.

i withdraw and reposition if it's still pumping. no waiting, no hoping it comes back in range. out, reassess, redeploy.

that's why the third position held for 6 minutes and made $149. the first two were quick cycles, the third one caught a bigger move and i let it run until the same exit rule triggered.

but this strat needs real self-control. the moment you start thinking "let me hold a bit more" or "it'll come back in range" you've already broken the logic.

the whole edge is in executing the exit exactly when the condition hits, not when it feels right.

if you don't trust yourself to exit on the rule, this strat won't work for you. it's that simple.

jawaban singkatnya: ngga.

framework-nya bisa gua build. tapi edge dari Meridian bukan dari framework-nya. edge-nya adalah dari lo sendiri.

tujuan agent ini adalah mindah otak dan flow lo dalam DLMM ke dalam agent itu. strategi, preferensi, cara milih token, cara nentuin range, cara lo exit. semua itu yang bikin agent kalian jadi kalian

mereka udah jago DLMM di Meteora, dan yang mereka lakuin adalah transfer ilmu mereka ke dalam agent ini. dari situ agent-nya jalan sendiri dengan cara pikir yang sama.

gua tanya ke orang-orang yang dapet $50 ke atas per hari dari agent ini. rata-rata mereka main dengan modal 2 SOL per posisi, maksimal 3 posisi jalan bersamaan.

kalau lo belum ngerti DLMM sama sekali, pelajarin dulu sebelum nyoba Meridian. bukan karena agent-nya susah, tapi karena lo ga bisa transfer ilmu yang lo belum punya.

the whataboutism that bitcoiners do around quantum is embarrassing to the industry 

while QCs are still very far out and while I think Bitcoin *will* be ready by then, it is an insane failure of basic brain function to equate bitcoin to a bank when it suits you but then say it's much superior to a bank when it doesn't 

i) bitcoin is secured by cryptography, banks are secured by trust. this is a cryptographic attack. you can not call the bitcoin support agent to freeze your shit, you are explicitly and unilaterally trusting cryptography 

ii) banks do not publicly publish pubkeys to be decrypted, bitcoin does

it's arguably the largest source of publicly decryptable financial data known to man 

iii) bank txns are controlled and reversible, bitcoin txns are not

iv) banks can push an update in seconds, bitcoin cannot

v) a bank has central monitoring systems, bitcoin does not (and for this attack, physically can not), an attack wouldn't even be attributable in a decentralized system and would be indistinguishable from random ppl misplacing their seed phrases

you can keep the attack going without ever being detected 

vi) a bank can take action on your account without you needing to do anything. this is much, much messier for a blockchain

a single ledger is infinitely easier to control than thousands of ledgers, that's kind of the entire point of this industry 

again, the qc risk is overblown forsure but assuming its absense is perhaps the biggest risk of all

alasannya simpel: lebih murah dan cukup banget buat jalanin Meridian dan agent-trenching gua sehari-hari.

dengan $10 sebulan, agent gua sekarang bisa hasilin sekitar $50 per hari. 

bukan karena model-nya mahal, tapi karena agent-nya udah "belajar" dari kesalahan sebelumnya dan adapt sama kondisi market.  otak yang lebih murah bisa perform bagus kalau data dan lesson-nya bener.

sebelumnya gua pakai OpenRouter dengan pay-per-use. buat satu agent itu masih oke dan lebih fleksibel. tapi karena gua udah multi-agent dan request-nya mulai banyak, biaya pay-per-use mulai ga efisien.

gua switch ke Minimax token plan yang baru diluncurin. max 1500 request per 5 jam. dari data usage gua di OpenRouter, per 24 jam gua ngabisin sekitar 2000 request buat 2 agents.

oh dan gua juga pakai Minimax buat ngoding tipis-tipis kalau Claude lagi di peak hour buat ngirit limit.

$10 sebulan buat agent yang hasilin 50$ per hari. math-nya gut.

We were losing users until we made this change.

Previously, we used our own built-in verification system on @NOMADZxyz.

However, many users were hesitant to connect their wallets, which created friction during the verification process.

We stayed in this situation for a while, but when we were preparing to onboard the @MonkeDAO community, they also expressed concerns about our verification method and the safety of their community members.

To solve this issue they recommended @MatricaLabs, as they were already partners with them.

As Solana SMB Gen 2 and Gen3 holders, we were able to secure a 75% discount on the Matrica Enterprise package.

Since then, our users can verify their NFTs off-chain using Discord, Telegram, and other platforms, without needing to connect their wallets directly.

reason: Perps / Trading cluster linked by [volume, sol, trading, pool, chart]

sampleTweets.text:
trashcan-sol, twice. $145 in 1 minute 8 seconds. $83 in 2 minutes 12 seconds. not my usual play but this volume was too good to ignore.

the setup: single side sol, 1 bin only. that's it.

but before anything else you need the right pool. this only works on 100/5 or 125/10 pools. anything with a 2% base fee won't give you enough to make this worthwhile.

the mechanic is simple. when your 1 bin fills and then fills back out, you're capturing roughly 2x the base fee in that single movement. on a 5% base fee pool that's around 10%, on a 10% pool you're looking at 20%+ in a single fill cycle. that's what the cards show.

volume needs to be in the 100k-200k per 5 minutes range for the bin to cycle fast enough. below that and you're just sitting there waiting.

the catch: you need to be completely locked in. eyes on the chart the whole time. when the bin fills you need to know immediately, and when it fills back out you're done, close and move on. this is not a set-and-forget play. one distraction and you miss the exit.

also prepare your mental before trying this. watching a single bin move in real time while $100+ is sitting in it is a different experience from holding a normal range position.

$228 in under 4 minutes total. gut strat for the right market conditions.

sampleTweets.text:
SIMD-420

Quantum-Assisted Optimal Transaction Ordering for Blockchains

We propose a transaction ordering framework in which block construction is formulated as a constrained global optimization problem and solved via quantum-assisted computation. Rather than applying heuristic or first-come-first-served sequencing, validators collect a batch of candidate transactions for each block and encode ordering, execution dependencies, and state transitions into a formal optimization model. The objective function captures protocol-defined goals such as fairness, maximal execution efficiency, minimal slippage, reduced extractable value (MEV), and adherence to risk and resource constraints. A quantum (or hybrid quantum-classical) optimizer is then used to compute a candidate ordering and execution plan that maximizes this objective under strict validity constraints.

To preserve determinism and consensus safety, all optimization outputs are verified by a classical deterministic validator, ensuring that resulting state transitions are valid, constraints are satisfied, and outcomes are reproducible. The protocol separates fairness specification (encoded in constraints and objective) from solution search (performed by the optimizer), preventing arbitrary or opaque decision-making. In the event of solver failure or disagreement, validators fall back to a canonical deterministic ordering rule, guaranteeing liveness.

This design transforms block production from a local, adversarial ordering problem into a globally optimized batch execution process. By leveraging quantum-assisted search for combinatorial optimization, the system aims to reduce MEV, improve capital efficiency, and enforce formally defined fairness policies, while maintaining verifiability, consensus compatibility, and robustness under heterogeneous validator capabilities.

Anza dropped constellations, a multi-proposer protocol for Solana with 50ms cycles.

Multiple validators compete to include your transaction every 50 milliseconds, so no single one can block or reorder it for profit

No more single-leader monopoly. Censorship resistance baked into the protocol itself.

MEV mitigation without trusting any third party.

This is what decentralized NASDAQ actually looks like at the infra level

a medium of exchange is not the defining characteristic of money

money is not what you transact in but what you think in

random thought that I just got after looking up money in the dictionary

the way you learn about money is through the barter problem (which is incorrect btw)

that is, I have potatoes and you have corn, and if I want corn and you want potatoes, then we trade

but if I don't want corn and you don't want potatoes, then we can't trade. therefore, we designate a medium of exchange and then use that for all other assets as well

the coincidence of double wants problem solved!

but now imagine you have tokenized all assets onchain and you have a colossal AMM that has all pairs

i.e corn/wheat, wheat/potatoes, potatoes/oil, oil/steel

ok now you have also solved the coincidence of double wants, you can trade any asset for any other asset

but there's still a problem, you can't make denominate rates consistently and thus you can't coordinate the economy

also, any asset that is not seasonal will not lose value with time and thus will get a monetary premium (hence gold) which will skew the price signal

therefore, the main functions must be i) unit of account, ii) store of value with iii) medium of exchange being a secondary function, in that order

or more succinctly, money is not the thing you transact in but the thing you think in

source: I made it up
